name: Black Coffee
address_note: Inside Vivian apartment complex clubhouse, Capital View

parking.description: Plenty of parking, 2 hours free

ambiance.description: Industrial and modern. Hip hop music, BET on TV, art by Black artists on sale. A celebration of Black culture.

quality.description: Best hot coffee in a while. Croissant was the worst pastry of the series.

seating: Stools at bar, community table, couches, large patio with limited outdoor tables
workNotes: Great place to work from home if you don't have any meetings
drinkNotes: Sweet and perfectly balanced
foodNotes: Worst pastry of the series — like a sponge. Large food and drink menu with seasonal options.

name: Daily Dose Coffee

parking.description: Plenty of free street parking and a free parking garage

ambiance.description: Dark and moody. Wall of coffee table books, illustrated cards/prints, housewares for sale.

quality.description: Salted maple brown sugar latte was sweet but balanced. Almond croissant from Alon's was good.

seating: Limited indoor seating, standing bar with outlets at every seat, a few patio tables
outlets: Outlets at every seat along the standing bar
workNotes: Odd lighting glare can be an issue during Zoom meetings
drinkNotes: Sweet but balanced
foodNotes: Heated, good quality. Limited pastry options. A bit expensive.

name: Academy Coffee

parking.description: Plenty of free street parking, though further walk if arriving later

ambiance.description: Outdoor-only seating. Noisy — leaf blowers, dogs, people talking. Small sundry store inside.

quality.description: Good espresso but couldn't taste the specialty flavors. Breakfast sandwich was overrated at $12.

seating: No indoor seating — grab-and-go only. Two indoor benches for waiting.
workNotes: Not conducive to working — grab-and-go only with no indoor seating
drinkNotes: Rose, mint, almond — good espresso base but couldn't taste the flavors
foodNotes: Beautiful but overrated. Only vanilla or chocolate syrups. Small seasonal menu.

name: Mr. Tombstones
address_note: OTP — outside the perimeter. Inside a renovated house.

parking.description: Small free dedicated lot with overflow and street parking. Warning: towing across the street Mon-Fri.

ambiance.description: Halloween year-round theme with incredible attention to detail. Spooky decor, multiple rooms with different vibes. Very community-oriented.

quality.description: Incredibly diverse seasonal menu. Famous Skull Muffins. Sweet potato iced latte was subtle and well-spiced.

seating: Outdoor patio with mismatched seating, multiple indoor rooms with different vibes
workNotes: Multiple rooms to choose from for different vibes
drinkNotes: Cream cheese cold foam, subtle sweet potato, nutmeg, pumpkin pie spice
foodNotes: Incredibly diverse seasonal menu. Weekly workshops, classes, and events.
address: 1087 S Marietta Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060

name: The Salty

parking.description: Multiple paid lots/garages (sometimes free hour depending on day/time). Some free street parking.

ambiance.description: Industrial style — exposed lights, dark colors. Small interior, more designed for grab-and-go. Cozy.

quality.description: Gourmet donuts are the star. Cookie butter latte was very sweet. Original glaze donut had perfect texture.

seating: Small interior, all seating comfortable with outlets at tables and barstools
workNotes: Small space but comfortable seating with outlets
drinkNotes: Very sweet, like Trader Joe's cookie butter spread
foodNotes: Original glaze (perfect texture, favorite), chocolate cake (dry), strawberry shortcake (jam + mascarpone), butterscotch crunch (massive, butter beer vibes)

name: Aurora Coffee

parking.description: Large free dedicated parking lot

ambiance.description: Quirky, fun, grunge. Wall of concert posters, floating bike, mural, artsy vibe.

quality.description: Iced Kodiak was sweet but not overly so. Own iced coffee flavors with option to make it 3x stronger.

seating: Decent indoor seating (room for more), intimate patio with lots of tables
workNotes: Decent indoor seating with intimate patio
drinkNotes: Chocolate and caramel with oat milk — sweet but not overly so. Option to make it 'grisly' (3x stronger).
foodNotes: Has food options. Bare menu with own iced coffee flavors.

name: Condesa Coffee

parking.description: Small lot with a few dedicated spaces. Lots of free street parking.

ambiance.description: Small, cozy interior with funky vibes. Super friendly baristas. Built for locals. Outdoor seating only (gorgeous).

quality.description: Seasonal passion fruit matcha looked promising but flavor was off.

seating: Only outdoor seating (gorgeous). No indoor seating — takeaway counter only.
workNotes: No indoor seating, no bathroom. Takeaway counter only.
drinkNotes: Color was promising but flavor was off
foodNotes: Very limited food options. Coffee membership available.
address: 480 John Wesley Dobbs Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30312

name: Necessary Purveyor

parking.description: Ponce City Market lot. Parking garage across street at Krog. Paid street parking. Got a parking ticket.

ambiance.description: Beautiful, aesthetically pleasing, bougie. Saxophone music. Clean and curated.

quality.description: Pistachio croissant from Alon's was the star. Cheese Danish was incredible. Iced mocha was decent.

seating: Lots of seating inside
workNotes: Gets difficult around noon when it fills up
drinkNotes: Decent and chocolatey
foodNotes: Thick pistachio paste — star of the show. Cheese Danish was possibly best ever. Pastries not heated. Also has refrigerated items, dry goods, sandwiches, salad bar.
address: 639 Glen Iris Dr NE, Atlanta, GA 30308

name: Javavino
address_note: Also a wine bar at night

parking.description: Small free lot shared with monastery. Large lot next door belongs to tavern (risky to use).

ambiance.description: Funky treehouse vibes. Felt like somebody's living room. Also a wine bar at night.

quality.description: Beasting Latte (vanilla/honey) was great. Red Iguana (raspberry/white chocolate) was odd. Long wait times.

seating: Few patio tables with fans, casual dining area inside, plenty of outlets
workNotes: Plenty of outlets. Long wait for drinks (10+ min with only 2 customers).
drinkNotes: Vanilla and honey — great
foodNotes: Dense, sticky, dry. Decent pastry selection plus full breakfast menu. Family-owned for 6 generations — beans grown at their estate in Nicaragua (seed to cup).
address: 579 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30307

name: Bloom Coffee Co

parking.description: Small apartment lot to the side, 3 hours validation

ambiance.description: Industrial with natural light and plants. Cute personal touches, little library, basic merch.

quality.description: Ordered matcha cloud but got lychee lemonade due to machine issue — tasted like soap. Croughnut was incredible.

seating: Larger inside than it appears. Limited outdoor seating. Outlets scattered around (wall, booth, bar, countertop).
outlets: Scattered around — wall, booth, bar, countertop
workNotes: Good amount of outlets scattered throughout
drinkNotes: Machine issue led to wrong drink. Lychee lemonade had hibiscus floral tea base — tasted like soap.
foodNotes: Lemon ice box flavor — possibly favorite pastry of the series. Spongy texture with tart lemon curd.

name: Portrait Coffee

parking.description: Giant dedicated free parking lot

ambiance.description: Simply decorated. Art from local artists on display and purchasable. Family photos at counter. Records playing.

quality.description: Aunt Viv Latte was spicy like a chai. House-made syrups. Chocolate croissant was extremely flaky but dry.

seating: Few patio tables. Outlets along booth and bar top.
workNotes: Outlets available along booth and bar top
drinkNotes: Cardamom — spicy, tasted like a spicy chai. House-made syrups.
foodNotes: Extremely flaky but dry. Brought in from elsewhere, heated up.
address: 1065 Ralph David Abernathy Blvd SW, Atlanta, GA 30310

name: El Premio

parking.description: Large paid lot outside. Recommended to find free residential street parking nearby.

ambiance.description: Expensive feeling. Light jazz and bossa nova. Warm lighting, surprisingly comfortable.

quality.description: Ube matcha was subtle and not overly sweet. Pistachio croissant at $9 was messy and overly sweet.

seating: Lots of outdoor seating. Large indoor seating area.
workNotes: Fills up around 11. Expensive but surprisingly comfortable.
drinkNotes: Not overly sweet, subtle ube flavor like cereal milk
foodNotes: Exploded, white chocolate coating overly sweet, not heated, only one napkin. Full breakfast menu.

name: Perk

parking.description: Plenty of free street parking and neighborhood parking

ambiance.description: Great branding. Cute, open, airy, inviting, colorful. Lively baristas. Soft hop music.

quality.description: First perfect score of the series. Happy Camper was chocolatey and delicious. Breakfast crunch wrap was amazing.

seating: Lots of outdoor seating (picnic area, alcove, under awning). Outlets everywhere.
workNotes: Outlets everywhere. Fills up around 10am.
drinkNotes: S'mores flavor mocha, half-caf, oat milk — chocolatey and delicious
foodNotes: Eggs, hash brown, cheese, tangy sauce like Cane's — amazing. Savory breakfast is the draw.

name: Sweet Hut
address_note: Also locations in Marietta, Lilburn, Duluth, Doraville

parking.description: Parking garage with 2-hour validation. Confusing to find — pass the shop, turn right, right again.

ambiance.description: Asian cafe and bakery. Inspired the whole coffee shop review series.

quality.description: Huge selection of buns. Chocolate Nutella bun was rich and a favorite. Lychee black tea with boba was great.

seating: Plenty of seating. Outlets around bar and booths.
workNotes: Fills up around 1:30pm. Outlets around bar and booths.
drinkHighlight: Lychee Black Tea with Lychee Jelly & Boba
drinkNotes: 2 free toppings included
foodNotes: Rich, favorite bun. Also: mozzarella bun, taro bun (dense/mochi), Mexican cream bun (coffee-flavored). Huge bakery selection plus sandwiches.

name: Michael Machi Cafe

parking.description: Huge free parking lot

ambiance.description: Busy and crowded. Cute decor. Specializes in matcha and soft serve.

quality.description: Banana pudding matcha latte was creative — not too sweet, banana flavor takes over when mixed.

seating: Seating available but not comfortable. Sushi boat section.
workNotes: NOT a place to get work done — loud, few outlets, not comfortable
drinkNotes: Dollop of banana pudding and wafers on top. Not too sweet, matcha neutral, banana flavor takes over.
foodNotes: American and Asian flavors like black sesame and ube.

name: Momo Cafe

parking.description: Large parking garage next door with 2-hour validation. Paid parallel street parking.

ambiance.description: Nice natural light. Coffee shop tucked under staircase with cafe seating upstairs overlooking highway.

quality.description: Okinawa brown sugar latte fell flat. Caramelized onion-gruyere croissant had incredible flavor but was super dense.

seating: Small upstairs area, not comfy. Overlooks highway. Couple of outlets on booth.
workNotes: Seating not comfortable, overlooks highway, limited outlets
drinkNotes: Fell flat with almond milk
foodNotes: Incredible flavor but super dense, not flaky, not heated, felt refrigerated. Listed by Infatuation as one of best in America.

name: Ash Coffee

parking.description: No dedicated lot. Plenty of street parking.

ambiance.description: General store feel. Tons of items for sale. Quirky, fun decor.

quality.description: Blueberry matcha was delicious. Pistachio croissant was light and flaky. One of the strongest breakfasts of the series.

seating: Some inside, patio (not super cozy but lots of seats). Outlets along bar and wall.
workNotes: Outlets along bar and wall. Patio seating available.
drinkNotes: Delicious — blueberry came through, not too sweet. Not for strong/bitter matcha lovers.
foodNotes: Light, flaky, thick pistachio paste (a bit mushy). Good selection of sweet, savory, and pastries. One of the strongest breakfasts of the series.

name: Thinkk
address_note: Shares space with apartment leasing office

parking.description: Plenty of free street parking

ambiance.description: Huge space. Gorgeous area with giant water feature. Modern without feeling cold. LGBT-friendly decor. Merch and knick-knacks.

quality.description: Almond chai latte was delightfully spicy. Blueberry cream cheese scone had great flavor but gummy texture.

seating: Huge space with plenty of seating. Large workspace area with outlets and a printer. Dog beds under desks.
outlets: In the workspace area (few and far between elsewhere)
workNotes: Dedicated workspace area with outlets and a printer. Dog beds under desks. Few outlets outside the workspace.
drinkNotes: Delightfully spicy with an extra kick
foodNotes: Heated. Great flavor but gummy/chewy inside — wrong texture. All treats made in-house.

name: Green Beans ATL

parking.description: No dedicated lot. Plenty of free street neighborhood parking.

ambiance.description: Small but well-used space. Cozy neighborhood feel that chain shops fail to replicate.

quality.description: Iced chai was great and full of spices. Raspberry cheese twist was flaky and ooey-gooey.

seating: Surprising amount, all comfortable. Cozy alcove with outlets lining a gigantic booth.
outlets: Lining the gigantic booth in the alcove
workNotes: Cozy alcove booth with outlets is perfect for working
drinkNotes: Great, full of spices
foodNotes: Heated, flaky, ooey-gooey raspberry sweetness. Decent pastry selection. Also offers salads.

name: San Francisco Coffee

parking.description: Giant free parking lot out back. Extra street parking on side. Parking lot across street.

ambiance.description: Possibly the coziest coffee shop ever. Exposed brick, dark beams, twinkle lights. Felt like Christmas.

quality.description: Lavender matcha was grassy and bitter. Cinnamon knot had perfect texture and flaky layers.

seating: Plenty of seating — tables, private alcove, outdoor patio
workNotes: Plenty of seating options including a private alcove
drinkNotes: Grassy and bitter — needed sweetener. Not her cup of tea.
foodNotes: Perfect texture, flaky layers, perfectly moist. Multitude of pastries. Full breakfast sandwich menu.
address: 1192 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306

name: East Pole

parking.description: Pretty large shared lot with 2-hour parking validation

ambiance.description: Sparse — tables, decor, selection. No frills. Only vanilla syrup and honey. But excellent hospitality.

quality.description: Vanilla latte was fine, nothing special. Queenamon pastry was really good with blueberry jam.

seating: Available but only a few outlets scattered around
workNotes: Remote workers paradise — pretty much everyone had laptops. Gets packed so arrive early.
drinkNotes: Fine, nothing special. Nice cup aesthetic.
foodNotes: Croissant-like pastry with flaky layers filled with blueberry jam — really good
address: 676 N Highland Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30306

name: Harbor Coffee

parking.description: Row of free street parking outside. Plenty of free daytime street parking in Inman Park.

ambiance.description: Cozy, relaxed, industrial, living room feel. Sparse knick-knacks. Most pleasant baristas. Soft pop music.

quality.description: Blueberry oat milk matcha couldn't taste blueberry or matcha but still sweet. Lemon pound cake was dense and moist.

seating: Spacious with couches, barstools, dining tables (not all comfortable). Free 2-hour WiFi.
workNotes: Free 2-hour WiFi. Not a lot of outlets. Fills up and gets popular.
drinkNotes: Couldn't taste blueberry or matcha but still sweet
foodNotes: Dense, moist, lemon flavor from icing. Limited but reasonably priced.

name: OPO Coffee

parking.description: Free 2-hour street parking a block away. 15-minute free parking right outside.

ambiance.description: Quintessential coffee shop. Chatter and light bossa nova. Window into the roastery where you can see beans being roasted.

quality.description: Earl Grey Ube Latte was vibrant purple and tasted like cereal milk/Froot Loops — highly recommended. Scone was a bit bland.

seating: Spacious and bright. Plenty of seating. Abundance of outlets.
workNotes: Easy to focus. Many remote workers. Abundance of outlets. Possibly favorite coffee shop of the series.
drinkNotes: Vibrant purple. Tasted like cereal milk / Froot Loops. Highly recommended.
foodNotes: Sugary crust on top, a bit bland, messy. Cookies, scones, muffins around $4.

name: Stereo

parking.description: Medium-sized shared lot. Additional parking two blocks away.

ambiance.description: Hip office lobby vibes. Minimal yet impactful decor. Record player music but dead silent otherwise. Also a bar at night.

quality.description: Best matcha ever had ($8.50 with oat milk and white mocha syrup). All out of pastries with very limited selection.

seating: Huge, open, airy inside. Outdoor patio. Outdoor bar area. Outlets available.
workNotes: Most people were working. Very quiet — dead silent.
drinkHighlight: Matcha Latte with Oat Milk & White Mocha
drinkNotes: Best matcha ever had. $8.50.
foodNotes: All out of pastries. Very limited food/drink selection.

name: Joe's Coffee

parking.description: Plenty of free street parking

ambiance.description: Curated hipster. Intentionally mismatched. Art on floors/ceilings/walls. Bookshelf, mugs to use, record player. Eerie sinister-vibe music.

quality.description: Vanilla latte couldn't taste vanilla. Potato scallion empanada was flavorful with chimichurri.

seating: Lots of seating, mostly empty. People working from home.
workNotes: Lots of seating, mostly empty. People working from home.
drinkNotes: Fine but couldn't taste vanilla
foodNotes: Tasted like a samosa, flavorful, with chimichurri. Limited selection — most pastries were empanadas. Sweet and savory options.
address: 510 Flat Shoals Ave SE, Atlanta, GA 30316

name: Little Tart Bakery
address_note: Doubles as a bar later in the day

parking.description: Huge free parking lot right outside

ambiance.description: Gorgeous interior. Doubles as a bar later in the day.

quality.description: Salted honey oat latte was great. Gluten-free lemon blueberry scone was dense, filling, and flavorful.

seating: Tables, booths, bar seating, outdoor patio — plenty of places to sit
workNotes: Fills up quickly and gets loud
drinkNotes: Salt put it over the edge — great
foodNotes: Dense, filling, full blueberries, lemon cuts through nicely. Incredible baked goods selection with abundance of gluten-free options. Reasonably priced $4-$8. Brunch menu on weekends.
